methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PA) using liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) to determine their concentration and validate the method's accuracy.

steps.description: PA single component analysis on 42 PA/PANO was performed with a 6490 tandem mass spectrometer coupled to a 1290 ultra-high-performance liquid chromatography system.
Chromatographic separation was carried out on a 150 mm × 2.1 mm i.d., 1.7 µm particle-sized Acquity CSH C18 UPLC column using specific eluents.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chromatographic separation using UPLC column

steps.description: Gradient elution started at 2.5% eluent B, increased to 10% at 15.5 min, 23% at 20.5 min, 36% at 25.0 min, and 100% at 28.0 min.
steps.searchable_summary: perform gradient elution for PA separation

steps.description: The Jet Stream electrospray ion source was operated in positive mode with specific settings for capillary voltage, nebulizer pressure, and gas flow.
steps.searchable_summary: configure electrospray ion source for PA analysis

steps.description: An external matrix-matched calibration employing 7-point calibration curves was performed to determine LOD and LOQ.
steps.searchable_summary: perform matrix-matched calibration for LOD and LOQ

steps.description: Recovery values were determined by spiking a blank matrix with known amounts of PA(NO) at different levels.
steps.searchable_summary: determine recovery values by spiking matrix

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and microscopic analysis of pollen samples to identify and classify pollen grains from different plant families, particularly focusing on PA-producing plants.

steps.description: The samples were brought up to room temperature, homogenized with a mortar.
An aliquot of 100 mg homogenized bee pollen was weighed into a 50-mL tube and mixed with 10 mL demineralized water.
After adding a drop of dish soap, the mixture was shaken by hand for 1 min.
An aliquot of 10 µL was transferred to an object carrier, dried for 30 min, and covered with Kaiser's glycerol gelatin for microscopy.
Three hundred pollen grains per sample were manually counted under a light microscope.
Pollen grains were classified into known PA producers and other plant families.
steps.searchable_summary: classify pollen grains by plant family
